plainEnglish: Unique people who saw the ad at least once.
whyItMatters: The base reach figure; 'n+' reach lets you plan to an effective-frequency goal (how many people saw it enough to remember).

plainEnglish: A fifteen-second TV commercial.
whyItMatters: A common length - cheaper than a 30s and good for frequency, but less room to build a story.

plainEnglish: Unique people who saw the ad at least twice.
whyItMatters: A minimum-exposure lens - many planners treat 2+ or 3+ as the point where a message starts to land.

definition: Audience exposed at least three times
plainEnglish: Unique people who saw the ad at least three times.
whyItMatters: A common effective-frequency target - planning to 3+ reach aims for enough repetition to be remembered.

plainEnglish: A thirty-second TV commercial - the classic TVC length.
whyItMatters: Enough time to build a story and emotion; the benchmark length most brand TV is bought and rated against.

definition: Video viewed for at least 3 seconds
plainEnglish: A video viewed for at least 3 seconds - a common 'counts as a view' threshold.
whyItMatters: A low bar; useful for reach but weak as a 'watched it' signal.

plainEnglish: A six-second short-format video ad (e.g. YouTube bumper).
whyItMatters: Built for unskippable, mobile, high-frequency reach - one idea, fast.

definition: Australian Business Number used for invoicing and contracts

plainEnglish: How often your ad showed in the very first position.
whyItMatters: The strongest position signal - important for brand terms and visibility.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Top and absolute top metrics

definition: Spend delivered faster early in flight
Media delivered but not yet invoiced

plainEnglish: Extra bits added to a search ad - sitelinks, callouts, phone numbers, prices. Now called 'assets' in Google Ads.
whyItMatters: Free extra real estate and information that lifts CTR; Google renamed them 'assets' but the function is the same.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About assets (formerly extensions)

plainEnglish: The type/shape of an ad - display banner, video, native, etc., with its own spec.
whyItMatters: Format shapes what the creative can do and where it runs; matching format to placement and objective is core to planning.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Display & mobile ad specs

definition: Group of ads within a break

plainEnglish: The score that decides whether and where your ad shows = bid x quality x context (plus thresholds).
whyItMatters: Explains why a higher bid alone doesn't guarantee top position - quality matters too.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About Ad Rank

definition: Budget set at ad set level

plainEnglish: Google's diagnostic (Poor to Excellent) of how well a responsive ad follows best practice.
whyItMatters: A guide, not a KPI - aim for Good/Excellent via variety and relevance, but don't chase it at the expense of the message.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About Ad Strength

plainEnglish: A standard event: an item was added to the cart.
whyItMatters: A mid-funnel intent signal in the shared Pixel/GA4 event vocabulary; powers optimisation and retargeting.

plainEnglish: Cost per 1,000 addressable (targeted) impressions.
whyItMatters: Addressable inventory costs more than broadcast - the premium buys precision, so judge it on outcomes.

plainEnglish: An umbrella term for data-driven, addressable TV buying across BVOD, CTV and addressable linear.
whyItMatters: The bridge between TV's reach and digital's targeting/measurement - increasingly how premium video is bought.

plainEnglish: Meta's highly automated shopping/sales campaign type (now branded Advantage+ Sales).
whyItMatters: Hands targeting, placement and creative to Meta's automation - strong for e-commerce, with less manual control.

plainEnglish: Performance partnerships that pay a commission for sales/leads they drive.
whyItMatters: Low-risk, pay-on-result reach via publishers and creators - but watch for last-click over-crediting.

plainEnglish: Meta's privacy-safe way to measure web events after Apple's ATT - events are ranked and reported in aggregate.
whyItMatters: Limits each domain to 8 prioritised events and removes user-level detail; shapes how iOS conversions are measured on Meta.

sources.label: Meta Business Help - Aggregated Event Measurement

plainEnglish: A column totalling every tracked conversion, including secondary ones not used for bidding.
whyItMatters: Useful for the full picture - but don't confuse it with the 'Conversions' column the algorithm optimises to.

definition: Ongoing campaign without fixed end date

plainEnglish: Average Order Value - average revenue per order.
whyItMatters: Lifts ROAS without touching media; rising AOV can make 'expensive' media profitable.
practitionerNotes: General ecommerce metric; no ad-industry canonical page.

plainEnglish: The width-to-height shape of a creative (e.g. 16:9 landscape, 9:16 vertical, 1:1 square).
whyItMatters: The wrong ratio gets cropped or letterboxed; vertical (9:16) is essential for mobile/social feeds and Stories/Reels.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Display & mobile ad specs

plainEnglish: A single creative file - a headline, image, logo, video or description.
whyItMatters: Automated formats (Performance Max, Advantage+, RSAs) assemble ads from many assets, so giving strong, varied assets matters more than designing one finished ad.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Performance Max assets

definition: Process of assigning credit to touchpoints
plainEnglish: How credit for a conversion gets shared across the ads a customer saw or clicked.
whyItMatters: The model you choose changes who looks like the hero - it's a lens, not the truth. Pair it with incrementality or MMM for causation.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: The time after a click or view within which a conversion still gets credited.
whyItMatters: Longer windows credit more conversions; comparing campaigns on different windows is apples-to-oranges.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About conversion windows

definition: Process determining ad eligibility and position
Bids evaluated per query in real time

plainEnglish: The movement of viewers between programs (inheritance, switching, tune-out).
whyItMatters: Helps explain why a slot delivers the audience it does - context for placement decisions.

plainEnglish: Google Ads automatically appends a GCLID to your click URLs so conversions can be tied back.
whyItMatters: Cleaner and richer than manual UTMs for Google Ads - the recommended way to connect Ads and Analytics.

plainEnglish: The mean number of people viewing across a program or time slot.
whyItMatters: The TV trading metric behind ratings - average audience as a share of the population is a rating.

plainEnglish: Advertising-supported Video On Demand - free streaming paid for by ads.
whyItMatters: The ad-funded streaming tier (incl. BVOD and ad tiers of the majors) - where streaming ad inventory lives.

plainEnglish: The expected result with no media - what would have happened anyway.
whyItMatters: Incrementality is everything above baseline; without it you'd credit ads for sales you'd have made regardless.

definition: Amount advertiser is willing to pay
Minimum acceptable price set by seller

plainEnglish: Of the eligible auctions, the share you actually bid on.
whyItMatters: A programmatic throttling/eligibility signal - are you entering the auctions you could?

definition: Month in which spend is recognised

plainEnglish: ROAS across all campaigns/channels together, not per-platform.
whyItMatters: The honest top-line - avoids the double-counting you get when each platform claims the same sale.

definition: Additional spots provided at no cost

plainEnglish: A survey-based study measuring an ad's impact on awareness, recall or consideration.
whyItMatters: How upper-funnel/brand work is measured - it won't show in last-click sales, so brand lift is the right yardstick.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About Brand Lift

definition: Triggers on related queries via intent
Approved spend limit for media or services
High-intensity spend period within a flight
Broadcaster Video On Demand in Australia
plainEnglish: Broadcaster Video On Demand - the streaming/catch-up apps of the TV networks (7plus, 9Now, 10 play, etc.).
whyItMatters: Premium, brand-safe, high-completion digital video; counted as part of Total TV under VOZ.

plainEnglish: Completed views divided by starts for broadcaster VOD video.
whyItMatters: Typically very high for BVOD - a key selling point of the channel.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Video Ad Completion Rate

plainEnglish: Cost per 1,000 BVOD impressions.
whyItMatters: BVOD commands a premium CPM vs open-web video - justified by completion and brand-safe content.

plainEnglish: Cost per BVOD video view.
whyItMatters: Video-view efficiency for broadcaster VOD inventory.

plainEnglish: A digital video ad served within broadcaster VOD.
whyItMatters: The delivery unit behind BVOD CPM/CPV and VOZ BVOD reach.

plainEnglish: Unique viewers reached via broadcaster video on demand.
whyItMatters: BVOD is counted as part of Total TV under VOZ, not a separate digital line.

plainEnglish: A clickable phone number on a search ad.
whyItMatters: Drives calls directly - valuable for service/local businesses.

plainEnglish: Short non-clickable benefit phrases under a search ad (e.g. 'Free shipping').
whyItMatters: Add selling points without using headline space.

plainEnglish: Meta's Conversions API - sends conversion events to Meta from your server, not just the browser pixel.
whyItMatters: Recovers signal lost to browser restrictions/ad blockers; the durable complement to the Pixel. Deduplicate against pixel events.

plainEnglish: On-demand replay of broadcast content after it airs (a core part of BVOD).
whyItMatters: Extends a program's audience beyond the live airing; measured within Total TV.

plainEnglish: Crediting a conversion to a click the user made.
whyItMatters: Click-based credit is the default and most trusted; view-based credit needs more scrutiny.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: A conversion that happened after the user clicked the ad.
whyItMatters: The 'they clicked then converted' path; the stronger, more defensible conversion signal.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: Measurement where events fire from the user's browser/app.
whyItMatters: Easy to set up but increasingly blocked (ITP, ad blockers, cookie limits) - a big driver of under-counted conversions.

sources.label: Google Tag Manager Help - Client vs server-side

plainEnglish: Measurement where events fire from the user's browser/app.
whyItMatters: Easy to set up but increasingly blocked (ITP, ad blockers, cookie limits) - a big driver of under-counted conversions.

sources.label: Google Tag Manager Help - Client vs server-side

plainEnglish: Campaign Manager 360 - Google's ad server for trafficking, tracking and measuring campaigns.
whyItMatters: The independent counting/serving layer that reconciles delivery across publishers and platforms.

definition: Percentage of media spend earned by agency

plainEnglish: Relative advertiser density on a keyword (low/medium/high).
whyItMatters: A competitiveness input from Keyword Planner - higher competition usually means higher CPCs.

plainEnglish: The group deliberately not shown the ads, used as the comparison in a test.
whyItMatters: The benchmark for causality - exposed-minus-control is the lift the advertising actually caused.

sources.label: Meta Business Help - About A/B testing

plainEnglish: A tracked outcome that counts as success - a sale, lead, signup or other key action.
whyItMatters: The thing campaigns optimise toward; how you define it (and which conversions count) shapes everything downstream.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: A specific outcome you've defined to track (purchase, lead, signup, call).
whyItMatters: What you set up and which actions you mark primary directly steers automated bidding.

plainEnglish: An experiment measuring the incremental conversions an ad caused (vs a holdout).
whyItMatters: Meta's randomised test (treatment vs holdout) - the causal answer to 'did the ads actually drive conversions?'

sources.label: Meta Business Help - About Conversion Lift

plainEnglish: The period after an ad interaction during which a conversion counts.
whyItMatters: Same idea as the attribution window - set it to your real purchase cycle and keep it consistent across reporting.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About conversion windows

plainEnglish: The written words in an ad - headlines, body, CTA.
whyItMatters: Clear, benefit-led copy does much of the persuading; the message matters as much as the design.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Responsive search ads

definition: Cost divided by landing page views
plainEnglish: Cost divided by landing-page views.
whyItMatters: A mid-funnel efficiency check between the click and the conversion.

plainEnglish: Cost per rating point (CPP) - the cost to buy one rating point of audience.
whyItMatters: The standard TV/radio cost-efficiency comparison across stations and dayparts.

plainEnglish: Reach expressed as a % of a defined universe or target.
whyItMatters: Used interchangeably with 'reach %' - always check the universe it's measured against.

plainEnglish: Cost per acquisition (per conversion).
whyItMatters: The bottom-line performance cost - match it to the conversion you actually care about.

plainEnglish: Cost per click.
whyItMatters: The core search/performance cost unit; tie it to conversion rate to judge true value.

plainEnglish: Cost per engagement.
whyItMatters: A social/content cost unit - useful upper-funnel, but engagement isn't an outcome on its own.

plainEnglish: Cost per lead.
whyItMatters: Lead-gen efficiency - but a cheap lead isn't always a good one, so track lead-to-sale quality too.

plainEnglish: Cost per 1,000 impressions.
whyItMatters: The standard cross-channel cost yardstick - but a cheap CPM can mean low-quality attention, so read it next to viewability and outcomes.

plainEnglish: Cost per rating point.
whyItMatters: How TV/radio cost-efficiency is quoted: CPP = Cost / GRPs.

plainEnglish: Cost per target rating point - CPP measured against the target audience.
whyItMatters: The 'against target' cost efficiency used in AU TV/radio buying.

plainEnglish: Cost per video view.
whyItMatters: Video efficiency - but 'view' thresholds differ by platform, so compare like-for-like.

definition: Ad asset including copy and visuals
plainEnglish: The actual ad - the copy, images, video and audio a person sees or hears.
whyItMatters: Creative is the biggest driver of effectiveness - a great plan with weak creative underperforms. It's the message, not just the media.

plainEnglish: Declining performance because the audience has seen the ad too many times.
whyItMatters: Rising frequency + falling CTR/CVR is the tell - the cue to refresh creative.

plainEnglish: Introducing new creative to replace tired ads.
whyItMatters: Combats fatigue/wearout - performance decays as the same audience sees the same ad too often.

plainEnglish: The rules for how multiple ads in a set are served (evenly, or optimised to the best).
whyItMatters: Optimise-for-best learns faster; even rotation gives cleaner read for testing. Pick to match your goal.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Responsive search ads

plainEnglish: Running multiple ad variants to see which performs best.
whyItMatters: The fastest, cheapest performance lever - creative differences usually beat targeting tweaks. Test one variable at a time.

plainEnglish: The point where an ad's impact falls off from repetition.
whyItMatters: Same idea as fatigue - the signal to rotate in fresh creative before efficiency erodes.

plainEnglish: Unique reach across linear TV and BVOD combined.
whyItMatters: A VOZ 'Total TV' lens - how AU TV reach is now planned across screens.

definition: Call to action button or prompt
plainEnglish: Call to action - the button or line telling the user what to do next ('Shop now', 'Learn more').
whyItMatters: A clear, single CTA lifts response; vague or competing CTAs cost conversions.

plainEnglish: CTA interactions divided by impressions - how often people engage the call to action.
whyItMatters: A creative-effectiveness signal for the action prompt; read with downstream conversions.

plainEnglish: Click-through rate - clicks divided by impressions, as a %.
whyItMatters: A diagnostic of creative/targeting relevance, not an outcome; inflated by bots and curiosity, so read with conversions.

plainEnglish: Connected TV - any TV connected to the internet streaming content (smart TVs, streaming sticks, consoles).
whyItMatters: Where streaming is watched; the device behind the shift from linear to on-demand viewing.

plainEnglish: The cumulative count of unique viewers/listeners over a period.
whyItMatters: Shows how audience builds over time - the basis of reach accumulation.

plainEnglish: Conversion rate - conversions divided by clicks (or visits), as a %.
whyItMatters: Where efficiency is won or lost after the click - landing page and offer quality.

definition: Cost for audience or data usage

plainEnglish: Removing double-counting when the same person is reached across multiple screens or placements.
whyItMatters: The whole point of currencies like VOZ - so a person seen on TV and BVOD counts once, giving true reach.

definition: Agreed trading terms between buyer and seller
Identifier used to transact a deal
Ability to spend budget and serve ads

plainEnglish: The supporting body text under the headline in an ad.
whyItMatters: Adds detail and the case to act; RSAs rotate up to 4.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Responsive search ads

definition: Demand Side Platform used to buy ads

plainEnglish: Audience overlap between two placements - people who saw both.
whyItMatters: High duplication means you're re-reaching the same people (more frequency, less reach).

plainEnglish: Display & Video 360 - Google's demand-side platform for programmatic buying across the open web and CTV.
whyItMatters: Where agencies run programmatic display, video, audio and CTV with deal management and frequency control.

plainEnglish: Automatically mixes and matches creative elements to find the best-performing combinations per person.
whyItMatters: Scales testing across audiences without building every variant by hand - the engine behind Advantage+ and PMax creative.

plainEnglish: Automatically drops the user's search term into your ad copy.
whyItMatters: Boosts relevance and CTR - but needs guardrails or it can produce awkward or off-brand text.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Dynamic Keyword Insertion

plainEnglish: Effective CPM - total cost normalised to a per-1,000 basis regardless of how you bought.
whyItMatters: Lets you compare a CPC buy and a CPM buy on the same scale.

definition: Blended realised rate after fees and discounts

plainEnglish: The reach that hit at least the minimum (effective) frequency to actually work.
whyItMatters: Raw reach over-counts people who saw it once and forgot; effective reach is the reach that counts.

plainEnglish: Total impressions that were available in the auctions you entered.
whyItMatters: The denominator behind impression share - your potential ceiling.

plainEnglish: Engagements divided by impressions (or reach), as a %.
whyItMatters: A relevance signal on social - but 'engagement' definitions vary by platform, so compare like-for-like.

plainEnglish: A Google feature that uses hashed first-party data (e.g. email) to recover conversions cookies miss.
whyItMatters: Improves measurement accuracy under signal loss while keeping data hashed/privacy-safe.

plainEnglish: Stopping the same conversion being counted twice when it's sent by both the pixel and the server (CAPI).
whyItMatters: Run pixel + CAPI together for resilience, but dedupe on a shared event_id or you double-count.

sources.label: Meta for Developers - Deduplicate pixel & server events

plainEnglish: The ranking that decides which of your 8 allowed Meta events gets reported when a user does several.
whyItMatters: Put your most valuable event (e.g. Purchase) top, or AEM may report a lesser action instead.

definition: Triggers on close variant exact queries

plainEnglish: A legacy fixed-format search ad (set headlines and descriptions).
whyItMatters: Retired - you can no longer create or edit them; RSAs replaced them. Useful to recognise in old accounts.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About Expanded Text Ads

plainEnglish: The group that did receive the ads in a test.
whyItMatters: Compared against the control group to isolate the incremental effect of the advertising.

sources.label: Meta Business Help - About A/B testing

plainEnglish: Free Ad-Supported Streaming Television - free, linear-style streaming channels (e.g. Samsung TV Plus).
whyItMatters: A fast-growing, ad-funded streaming format that recreates the channel-surfing experience online.

plainEnglish: Of ad requests, the share filled with an ad.
whyItMatters: A supply-side health metric - low fill means inventory isn't being monetised.

plainEnglish: The actual landing page a Google Ads click takes the user to.
whyItMatters: Separated from tracking parameters so measurement and destination can be managed independently.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Final URLs & tracking templates

plainEnglish: Gives 100% of the credit to the first ad the customer interacted with.
whyItMatters: Over-credits the opening touch. Note: Google retired first-click in Google Ads/GA4 - only last-click and data-driven remain there.
practitionerNotes: Retired in Google Ads and GA4 (rules-based models removed); still seen in some third-party tools.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Deprecated attribution models

plainEnglish: Ad-supported broadcast television that's free to watch (FTA).
whyItMatters: The backbone of mass reach in Australia; bought on VOZ ratings.

plainEnglish: On average, how many times each reached person saw the ad.
whyItMatters: Too low and the message doesn't land; too high wastes budget. Effective frequency is the sweet spot.

definition: Alias of Free To Air TV
Integrated upper mid and lower funnel approach

plainEnglish: Google Click Identifier - a unique tag Google adds to a click to track it through to conversion.
whyItMatters: The link between a Google Ads click and the conversion it drives; needed for accurate Ads/Analytics attribution and offline imports.

plainEnglish: Generative Engine Optimisation - optimising content to be surfaced and cited by AI answer engines.
whyItMatters: As people get answers from AI rather than clicking links, being referenced by generative engines is an emerging visibility battleground.

plainEnglish: A test that turns media on in some regions and holds it out in others to measure causal impact.
whyItMatters: Privacy-proof incrementality - no user tracking needed. Netflix and others use geo-holdouts to validate big budget bets.

plainEnglish: Gross Rating Points - total rating points bought = Reach % x Frequency.
whyItMatters: The headline 'weight' of a TV/radio campaign (duplicated audience).

definition: Goods and Services Tax in Australia

plainEnglish: Google Tag Manager - a tool to deploy and manage tags without editing site code each time.
whyItMatters: Lets marketers add/change tracking quickly and cleanly; the standard tag-management layer.

plainEnglish: The most prominent line of ad text - the first thing people read.
whyItMatters: Does most of the work in grabbing attention; RSAs use up to 15 to test combinations.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Responsive search ads

plainEnglish: Views retained past the midpoint - did the creative keep attention?
whyItMatters: A creative-diagnostic shorthand. Agency-defined, not an official platform metric.
practitionerNotes: Built from Meta's standard video milestone fields; no official Meta page.

plainEnglish: An audience deliberately excluded from seeing the ads, to measure incrementality against.
whyItMatters: The control side of a lift test - the difference between holdout and exposed is the causal lift.

sources.label: Meta Business Help - About Conversion Lift

plainEnglish: An experiment comparing a control (holdout) group against an exposed group.
whyItMatters: The standard design for proving advertising caused an outcome rather than just correlating with it.

sources.label: Meta Business Help - About Conversion Lift

definition: Views in first seconds divided by impressions
plainEnglish: Views in the first seconds divided by impressions - did the opening stop the scroll?
whyItMatters: A creative-diagnostic shorthand on social. Agency-defined, not an official platform metric - define it consistently internally.
practitionerNotes: Typically 3-second video plays / impressions. No official Meta page; built from standard video fields.

plainEnglish: Unique households exposed to the ad.
whyItMatters: A household-level reach view, used where the household (not the individual) is the buying unit.

plainEnglish: The % of available impressions you actually received.
whyItMatters: The clearest 'how much headroom is left' signal on search.

plainEnglish: The extra unique reach BVOD adds beyond linear TV.
whyItMatters: Quantifies how much new audience streaming brings to a TV plan.

plainEnglish: The extra unique people a new channel or placement adds on top of what you already reach.
whyItMatters: The core case for adding a channel - does it reach new people or just re-hit the same ones?

definition: Outcomes caused by media not correlation
plainEnglish: The outcomes that were actually caused by your media - not just correlated with it.
whyItMatters: The closest thing to ground truth: what happened with the ads vs a comparable group without them. Cuts through attribution's over-claiming.

plainEnglish: Creator-led promotion where a social personality features a brand to their audience.
whyItMatters: Borrows the creator's trust and reach; needs clear disclosure and brief alignment to work.

plainEnglish: A standard event: the checkout process was started.
whyItMatters: A high-intent funnel step; the gap to Purchase highlights checkout friction.

definition: Insertion Order used to book media
Key Performance Indicator used to measure success
plainEnglish: Key Performance Indicator - a metric chosen to measure success against a goal.
whyItMatters: Picking the right KPI per objective is half the battle - the wrong one makes good work look bad and vice versa.
practitionerNotes: General business term; no ad-industry canonical definition.

plainEnglish: The destination page actually loaded after a click.
whyItMatters: Stronger than a click (which can bounce before load); the click-to-LPV gap reveals lost traffic.

definition: 100% credit to final click before conversion
plainEnglish: Gives 100% of the credit to the very last ad clicked before converting.
whyItMatters: The default in most platforms; it over-credits the closing channel and undervalues upper-funnel work.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: The duration of a TV or video ad (e.g. 6s, 15s, 30s, 60s).
whyItMatters: Trades cost against storytelling room - and drives the rating weight (a 15s often buys at a discount to a 30s).

definition: Difference between exposed and control groups
plainEnglish: The difference in outcomes between an exposed group and a control group.
whyItMatters: The measured uplift caused by advertising - the core output of an incrementality test.
example: Exposed group converts at 5%, control at 4% = 1 point (25% relative) lift.

plainEnglish: An experiment that measures the incremental impact of advertising.
whyItMatters: The framework behind brand, conversion, sales and search lift - exposed vs control, measured causally.

plainEnglish: Splits the credit equally across every touchpoint in the path.
whyItMatters: Simple but treats a fleeting impression the same as a decisive click. Retired in Google Ads/GA4.
practitionerNotes: Retired in Google Ads and GA4; conceptual only there now.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Deprecated attribution models

plainEnglish: Traditional scheduled broadcast TV - you watch what's on, when it's on.
whyItMatters: Still huge for fast, broad reach; increasingly planned together with BVOD as 'Total TV' via VOZ.

definition: CTR measured on link clicks only
plainEnglish: CTR counting only link clicks.
whyItMatters: Closer to real site traffic than all-click CTR, which includes likes, expands and other interactions.

plainEnglish: Advertising on LinkedIn - targeted by job title, company, industry and seniority.
whyItMatters: The primary B2B social channel; premium CPMs but precise professional targeting.

plainEnglish: Watching a broadcast in real time as it airs.
whyItMatters: Drives appointment viewing and big simultaneous audiences (sport, finales) - premium for reach and attention.

plainEnglish: Shows your business address/map pin on a search ad.
whyItMatters: Drives foot traffic and signals local relevance.

definition: Impression share lost due to budget limits
plainEnglish: Impression share you missed because budget ran out.
whyItMatters: A direct signal to add budget - you're leaving impressions on the table.

definition: Impression share lost due to ad rank
plainEnglish: Impression share you missed because of low Ad Rank.
whyItMatters: Says improve bids/quality (not budget) to capture more impressions.

plainEnglish: Customer Lifetime Value - the total value a customer is worth over time.
whyItMatters: Justifies a higher acquisition cost - if LTV is high, a 'bad' CPA can still be very profitable.
practitionerNotes: General business metric; no ad-industry canonical page.

definition: Rule defining how keywords match queries
Media Booking Authority approving media spend
Document outlining channels budgets and deliverables

plainEnglish: Marketing Efficiency Ratio - total revenue divided by total marketing spend.
whyItMatters: A privacy-proof, whole-business efficiency read that's resilient to attribution gaps.
practitionerNotes: General business metric; no IAB/Google/Meta canonical page.

plainEnglish: Advertising across Facebook and Instagram (and Messenger) via Meta's ad platform.
whyItMatters: Massive reach plus first-party data and automation (Advantage+); a core performance and brand channel.

plainEnglish: Television audience measurement for Australia's metropolitan markets (OzTAM's metro panel).
whyItMatters: The metro half of TV measurement; combined with Regional TAM and BVOD into VOZ Total TV.

plainEnglish: Marketing Mix Modelling - top-down statistical modelling of sales against media spend and other factors.
whyItMatters: Privacy-proof and channel-spanning - great for budget allocation. Resurgent because it survives signal loss. Slow and broad-brush.

sources.label: Think with Google - Marketing mix modeling

plainEnglish: Measurement of Outdoor Visibility and Exposure - Australia's out-of-home audience currency.
whyItMatters: How OOH reach and frequency are planned and traded here; run under the Outdoor Media Association.

definition: Master Services Agreement governing the relationship

plainEnglish: Multi-Touch Attribution - spreading conversion credit across multiple touchpoints, user by user.
whyItMatters: Granular when it works, but increasingly limited by cookie loss and walled gardens. Triangulate with MMM and incrementality.

sources.label: Nielsen - A guide to multi-touch attribution

plainEnglish: Ads styled to match the look and feel of the content around them.
whyItMatters: Less interruptive and higher engagement, but must be clearly labelled as advertising.

plainEnglish: Total unique people exposed, counting each person once.
whyItMatters: The de-duplicated headline reach - stops you double-counting someone seen on TV and BVOD.

plainEnglish: The single metric that best captures the core value a business delivers.
whyItMatters: Aligns teams on one number - but a poorly chosen North Star can drive the wrong behaviour.
practitionerNotes: General product/business framework; no ad-industry canonical page.

plainEnglish: A conversion that happens off-site (a phone sale, an in-store purchase) uploaded back into the platform.
whyItMatters: Closes the loop on leads that convert offline, so bidding optimises to real sales not just form-fills.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Offline conversion imports

plainEnglish: Objectives and Key Results - a goal-setting framework pairing an ambition with measurable results.
whyItMatters: Keeps campaigns tied to business objectives rather than vanity activity.
practitionerNotes: General management framework; no ad-industry canonical page.

sources.label: Google re:Work - Set goals with OKRs

definition: Open exchange available to all buyers

plainEnglish: Opportunities To See - how many chances the average person had to see the ad.
whyItMatters: Frequency by another name, common in out-of-home and press planning.

plainEnglish: CTR counting only clicks that leave the platform.
whyItMatters: The closest social CTR to actual website visits.

plainEnglish: A native advertising and content-discovery platform (the 'recommended for you' modules on publisher sites).
whyItMatters: Scaled native reach across the open web; good for content distribution and mid-funnel.

plainEnglish: How often your ad ranked above a specific competitor's (Auction Insights).
whyItMatters: Competitive intelligence on the search results page.

definition: How often competitor appears with you
plainEnglish: How often a competitor's ad appeared in the same auction as yours (Auction Insights).
whyItMatters: Shows who you actually compete against on a keyword.

plainEnglish: Australia's official TV/video audience measurement body.
whyItMatters: Runs the panels and (with Regional TAM) the VOZ Total TV currency - the source of the ratings TV is traded on.

definition: Rate of spend delivery versus plan

plainEnglish: Paid advertising on social platforms (Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn, etc.).
whyItMatters: Scaled reach plus rich targeting and creative formats across the funnel.

plainEnglish: A recruited sample of households/people whose viewing is measured to represent the whole population.
whyItMatters: The backbone of TV/radio measurement - results are projected from the panel up to the population.

sources.label: ThinkTV - How do TV ratings work?

plainEnglish: Adjusting the panel sample so it matches the real population's makeup.
whyItMatters: Corrects for over/under-represented groups so projected audiences are accurate.

sources.label: ThinkTV - How do TV ratings work?

plainEnglish: Subscription television (e.g. Foxtel).
whyItMatters: Narrower reach than FTA but genre/audience concentration (sport, news, lifestyle).

plainEnglish: Advertising on Pinterest - high-intent, discovery-led, visual.
whyItMatters: Strong for retail, home, food and planning mindsets; users are often actively shopping for ideas.

definition: Tracking tag for conversions and audiences
plainEnglish: A small piece of tracking code on your site that records actions and builds audiences.
whyItMatters: The signal source for conversion tracking and retargeting - but browser-based pixels are weakened by signal loss (pair with server-side/CAPI).

definition: Purchase Order issued by a client

plainEnglish: How often a competitor ranked above you when you both showed (Auction Insights).
whyItMatters: A head-to-head position signal against named competitors.

definition: More credit to first and last touchpoints
plainEnglish: Gives most credit to the first and last touchpoints, less to those in between.
whyItMatters: Also called U-shaped. Retired in Google Ads/GA4; survives in some other analytics tools.
practitionerNotes: Retired in Google Ads and GA4.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Deprecated attribution models

plainEnglish: Shows products/services with prices under a search ad.
whyItMatters: Sets expectations and pre-qualifies clicks.

plainEnglish: The conversion action a campaign actually bids and optimises toward.
whyItMatters: Only primary conversions drive automated bidding - secondary ones are tracked but don't steer the algorithm.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: The main metric a decision is made on.
whyItMatters: Agreeing the primary metric up front stops post-hoc cherry-picking of whichever number looks good.
practitionerNotes: General goal-setting vocabulary; no canonical ad-industry page.

sources.label: Google re:Work - Set goals with OKRs

plainEnglish: The main body copy of a social ad (the text above the image/video on Meta).
whyItMatters: Often truncated - front-load the hook in the first line before the 'See more' cut.

definition: Automated buying and selling of digital media
plainEnglish: Automated buying and selling of digital ad inventory through technology (auctions or automated deals).
whyItMatters: A buying method, not a channel - display, video, audio, CTV and DOOH can all be bought programmatically.

plainEnglish: Display (banner) advertising bought programmatically.
whyItMatters: Scaled, targeted, cheap reach - but watch viewability and brand safety on the open web.

plainEnglish: Highlights a sale or offer on a search ad.
whyItMatters: Surfaces deals at the moment of intent.

plainEnglish: A standard event: a transaction was completed.
whyItMatters: The bottom-funnel conversion most performance campaigns optimise to.

plainEnglish: Google's 1-10 diagnostic of expected CTR, ad relevance and landing-page experience.
whyItMatters: A diagnostic, not a KPI - it influences Ad Rank and CPCs, but chase relevance, not the number.

plainEnglish: The % of a target audience watching at a given time (one rating = 1% of the target).
whyItMatters: The atomic unit of TV/radio trading; ratings sum to GRPs/TARPs.

plainEnglish: The number (or %) of unique people who saw your ad at least once.
whyItMatters: Reach is people; impressions are views. Reach vs frequency is the central planning trade-off.

plainEnglish: The incremental unique reach a channel or tactic adds beyond the base plan.
whyItMatters: Quantifies whether new spend reaches new people or just adds frequency to the same audience.

definition: Matching invoices to bookings and delivery
Updated delivery projection based on actuals

plainEnglish: Television audience measurement for Australia's regional markets.
whyItMatters: The regional counterpart to OzTAM's metro panel; feeds into VOZ Total TV.

definition: Ads sold by retailers using first-party data

plainEnglish: TV viewing data captured from set-top boxes and smart TVs at scale.
whyItMatters: Big-data viewing signals that complement panels - more granularity, used increasingly in modern TV measurement.

definition: When revenue is recognised for accounting

plainEnglish: Return on ad spend - revenue divided by ad spend.
whyItMatters: Headline performance efficiency, but platform ROAS uses that platform's attribution and over-counts; validate with incrementality.

plainEnglish: Return on investment - profit divided by cost, as a %.
whyItMatters: Unlike ROAS it nets out costs/margin. Clients often say 'ROI' but mean ROAS - clarify which.
practitionerNotes: General finance metric; no ad-industry canonical definition.

plainEnglish: Responsive Search Ad - you provide many headlines and descriptions and Google assembles the best combination.
whyItMatters: The default search ad format. Feed it varied, strong assets; pin sparingly so the system can optimise.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Responsive search ads

plainEnglish: Search Ads 360 - Google's enterprise platform for managing search campaigns across engines.
whyItMatters: Cross-engine (Google + Microsoft) search management and bidding for larger accounts.

plainEnglish: The part of a creative guaranteed to stay visible - keep key text and logos inside it.
whyItMatters: Platforms crop and overlay UI (captions, buttons) on video; anything outside the safe area can be cut off.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Display & mobile ad specs

plainEnglish: The incremental sales an ad caused, measured exposed vs unexposed (often via retail/panel data).
whyItMatters: Ties advertising to actual sales causally - the gold standard for FMCG and retail.

plainEnglish: Paid search advertising - text ads on search results pages.
whyItMatters: Intent-driven and measurable; usually the last click before conversion, which is why it over-credits in last-click reporting.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Create a Search campaign

plainEnglish: The rule deciding which clicks in a search path get conversion credit.
whyItMatters: Google now offers last-click and data-driven only (the rules-based models were retired) - the choice shifts credit between keywords.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

definition: Impact of spend changes on volume

plainEnglish: The time gap between a click and the conversion it leads to.
whyItMatters: Long lags mean today's conversions came from earlier clicks - judging a campaign too soon understates it.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

definition: Increase in search after TV exposure
plainEnglish: The increase in brand/product searches caused by ad exposure (often after TV/video).
whyItMatters: A useful proxy for upper-funnel demand creation - shows TV and video driving people to search.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About Search Lift

plainEnglish: Total impression share missed across budget and rank reasons.
whyItMatters: The combined gap between what you got and what was available.

formula_or_notes: Search,Point where more spend adds minimal value

definition: Actual user query that triggered ad

plainEnglish: The estimated number of monthly searches for a term.
whyItMatters: Demand sizing for keyword planning, from Keyword Planner.

definition: Winner pays next highest bid plus increment

plainEnglish: A conversion tracked for insight but not optimised toward.
whyItMatters: Lets you watch supporting actions (e.g. add-to-cart) without distracting the bidding algorithm.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About attribution models

plainEnglish: A supporting metric watched for context, not the basis of the decision.
whyItMatters: Guards against tunnel vision on the primary metric (e.g. watching quality alongside volume).
practitionerNotes: General goal-setting vocabulary; no canonical ad-industry page.

sources.label: Google re:Work - Set goals with OKRs

plainEnglish: Search Engine Marketing - paid ads on search results (i.e. paid search).
whyItMatters: Captures high-intent demand at the moment of search; the workhorse of lower-funnel performance.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Create a Search campaign

plainEnglish: Search Engine Optimisation - earning unpaid (organic) visibility in search results.
whyItMatters: Not bought media, but it shapes how much you must spend on paid search and how clicks convert.

plainEnglish: Measurement where events are sent from your server rather than the browser.
whyItMatters: More reliable and privacy-resilient; the modern answer to signal loss (sGTM, CAPI, server-side GA4).

sources.label: Google Tag Manager Help - Client vs server-side

plainEnglish: Measurement where events are sent from your server rather than the browser.
whyItMatters: More reliable and privacy-resilient; the modern answer to signal loss (sGTM, CAPI, server-side GA4).

sources.label: Google Tag Manager Help - Client vs server-side

plainEnglish: Viewing data collected from cable/satellite/IPTV set-top boxes.
whyItMatters: A form of return-path data; large-scale but needs careful handling (which box, who's watching) before it's a currency.

plainEnglish: Server-side Google Tag Manager - runs tags on a server you control instead of in the browser.
whyItMatters: More durable, privacy-resilient measurement (first-party context, less data sent client-side) as cookies/pixels weaken.

sources.label: Google for Developers - Server-side Tag Manager

plainEnglish: A program's share of everyone watching TV at that moment.
whyItMatters: Different from rating (share is of viewers; rating is of the population).

plainEnglish: Your advertising presence as a share of the category's total.
whyItMatters: Excess share of voice vs market share is a classic growth predictor.

plainEnglish: Extra clickable links under a search ad pointing to specific pages.
whyItMatters: Send people to the most relevant page and take up more of the results space.

definition: Service Level Agreement defining response times

plainEnglish: Advertising on Snapchat - full-screen vertical video and AR formats.
whyItMatters: Reaches younger audiences; AR lenses are a distinctive, high-engagement format.

definition: Statement of Work defining scope and deliverables

plainEnglish: A publisher's technical requirements for a creative - sizes, file types, durations, weights.
whyItMatters: Build to spec or the ad gets rejected. Specs change, so always work from the current sheet.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Display & mobile ad specs

definition: Supply Side Platform used by publishers

plainEnglish: A header plus a list of values under a search ad (e.g. Brands: A, B, C).
whyItMatters: Previews your range/categories before the click.

plainEnglish: Subscription Video On Demand - paid streaming (Netflix, Disney+, etc.).
whyItMatters: Historically ad-free, but ad-supported tiers now open premium streaming inventory to advertisers.

definition: Terms and Conditions applying to services

plainEnglish: A native advertising and content-discovery platform, similar to Outbrain.
whyItMatters: Large open-web native footprint for content and performance campaigns.

plainEnglish: A snippet of script placed on a site/app to collect data or serve/track ads.
whyItMatters: The plumbing of measurement; managed centrally via a tag manager to avoid a mess of hard-coded scripts.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - The Google tag

plainEnglish: An omnichannel video/outstream advertising platform across premium publishers.
whyItMatters: Scaled outstream video in editorial environments - brand video beyond the walled gardens.

plainEnglish: A major independent demand-side platform (DSP) for programmatic buying.
whyItMatters: The leading non-walled-garden DSP; strong in CTV and the open internet via UID2 identity.

plainEnglish: A Meta metric: the video played to completion, or for at least 15 seconds.
whyItMatters: A more meaningful 'watched it' signal than a 3-second view for optimisation.

definition: Users stopping scroll to view video
plainEnglish: Share of users who stopped scrolling to watch the video.
whyItMatters: A creative-diagnostic shorthand. Agency-defined, not an official platform metric.
practitionerNotes: Often equated with hook rate (3s plays / impressions); no official Meta page.

plainEnglish: Advertising on TikTok via TikTok Ads Manager.
whyItMatters: Sound-on, creator-led video reach with strong cultural impact; rewards native, entertaining creative.

definition: Bid modifier by time of day

plainEnglish: Gives more credit to touchpoints closer in time to the conversion.
whyItMatters: Favours lower-funnel tactics. Retired in Google Ads/GA4 alongside the other rules-based models.
practitionerNotes: Retired in Google Ads and GA4.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Deprecated attribution models

plainEnglish: The estimated bid needed to show at the top of the search results.
whyItMatters: A planning input for what a keyword costs to compete on.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Top of page bid estimate

plainEnglish: How often your ad showed above the organic search results.
whyItMatters: A position proxy for search visibility and brand defence.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Top and absolute top metrics

plainEnglish: A reusable URL formula in Google Ads that defines tracking parameters separately from the landing page.
whyItMatters: Change tracking once at the template level without editing every ad's final URL.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - Final URLs & tracking templates

definition: Setting up ads and tags in platforms
Clear disclosure of supply and costs
Target return on ad spend strategy

plainEnglish: Linking TV ad exposure to downstream outcomes like site visits, searches or sales.
whyItMatters: Brings TV into the measurement conversation - usually via geo/holdout or spike analysis, not user-level tracking.

plainEnglish: An experiment measuring the incremental impact of TV advertising.
whyItMatters: Proves TV's contribution causally - important because TV never shows up well in last-click reporting.

plainEnglish: Unique people across placements/channels, counting each person once.
whyItMatters: The point of de-duplicated currencies like VOZ - cross-channel reach without inflation.

plainEnglish: CTR based on unique users rather than total clicks.
whyItMatters: Removes repeat clickers - a truer read of how many distinct people clicked.

plainEnglish: The total measurable population a survey or currency represents.
whyItMatters: The denominator behind reach and ratings - 'reach %' only means something against a defined universe.

sources.label: ThinkTV - How do TV ratings work?

plainEnglish: Tags added to a URL (source, medium, campaign, etc.) so analytics can attribute the visit.
whyItMatters: The manual backbone of campaign tracking in GA4 - consistent UTM conventions make or break reporting.

sources.label: Google Analytics Help - UTM / URL builder

plainEnglish: The UTM parameter naming the specific campaign (e.g. winter_sale).
whyItMatters: Ties visits and conversions back to the campaign that drove them.

sources.label: Google Analytics Help - UTM / URL builder

plainEnglish: The UTM parameter distinguishing creatives/links (e.g. video_a vs carousel_b).
whyItMatters: Lets you compare which creative or link variant drove the traffic.

sources.label: Google Analytics Help - UTM / URL builder

plainEnglish: The UTM parameter naming the channel type (e.g. cpc, paid_social, email).
whyItMatters: The 'how' - drives default channel groupings in GA4; inconsistent mediums break those groupings.

sources.label: Google Analytics Help - UTM / URL builder

plainEnglish: The UTM parameter naming where the traffic came from (e.g. google, meta, newsletter).
whyItMatters: The 'who sent them' - keep values standardised or your channel reporting fragments.

sources.label: Google Analytics Help - UTM / URL builder

plainEnglish: The UTM parameter for the keyword (mostly used in manual paid-search tagging).
whyItMatters: Largely superseded by auto-tagging/GCLID for Google search, but still used in some manual setups.

sources.label: Google Analytics Help - UTM / URL builder

plainEnglish: Revenue (or assigned worth) attributed to conversions.
whyItMatters: The numerator of ROAS - only as good as your conversion-value setup.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About conversion values

plainEnglish: Dynamic placeholders in a Google Ads URL that auto-fill click details (device, network, placement).
whyItMatters: Captures rich click context for reporting without manual tagging per ad.

plainEnglish: Cost per 1,000 viewable impressions (50% on screen for 1s+, per MRC).
whyItMatters: Pays only for impressions that could be seen - a fairer cost than raw CPM.

plainEnglish: A video played all the way to the end.
whyItMatters: The numerator of completion rate; a stronger watch signal than a view.

plainEnglish: Completed views divided by video starts, as a %.
whyItMatters: The headline video-quality metric; BVOD completion rates run very high, part of BVOD's pitch.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Video Ad Completion Rate

plainEnglish: A counted video play (the threshold varies by platform).
whyItMatters: 'View' means different things on different platforms - always check the threshold before comparing.

plainEnglish: Crediting a conversion to an ad impression that wasn't clicked.
whyItMatters: Values upper-funnel exposure, but inflates easily - treat view-through numbers with caution.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About view-through conversions

plainEnglish: A standard event: a product or key page was viewed.
whyItMatters: An upper-funnel signal for building retargeting audiences and seeding optimisation.

plainEnglish: A conversion credited to an ad that was seen (an impression) but not clicked.
whyItMatters: Captures display/video influence clicks miss - but easy to over-claim, so check the window and weighting.

sources.label: Google Ads Help - About view-through conversions

plainEnglish: The image or video element of an ad.
whyItMatters: The scroll-stopper on social and the emotional carrier on video - usually the single biggest lever on performance.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Display & mobile ad specs

plainEnglish: Australia's official Total TV measurement and trading currency - broadcast + BVOD, de-duplicated.
whyItMatters: Since Dec 2024 it's how TV is bought and evaluated here. If you plan or report TV in Australia, you quote VOZ.

plainEnglish: View-through (completion) rate - the share of video views that played to completion or a set point.
whyItMatters: A core video quality signal; 'view' definitions differ by platform, so check what counts before comparing.

sources.label: IAB Australia - Video Ad Completion Rate

definition: Increase in site visits after TV exposure
plainEnglish: The increase in site visits caused by ad exposure (often measured after TV bursts).
whyItMatters: Connects offline/upper-funnel media to on-site behaviour without relying on last-click.
practitionerNotes: Not a standardised named product; measured via geo/holdout or analytics correlation.

plainEnglish: Of the auctions you bid in, the share you won.
whyItMatters: A programmatic health metric - low win rate points to bid, pacing or eligibility issues.

definition: Video platform bought via Google Ads
plainEnglish: Video advertising on YouTube, bought through Google Ads / DV360.
whyItMatters: Huge video reach across screens (incl. CTV); formats from skippable to bumpers to non-skip.
